ddls:
CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the site where the data was collected */,
  "age" REAL /* Age of the subject */,
  "male" INTEGER /* Binary indicator of male gender */,
  "female" INTEGER /* Binary indicator of female gender */,
  "label" INTEGER /* Label indicating the diagnosis type (0: NC, 1: MCI, 2: AD) */,
  "consent_date" TEXT /* Date when the subject provided consent */,
  "diagnosis_date" TEXT /* Date when the subject was diagnosed */,
  "education_level" TEXT /* Education level of the subject */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the subject */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation of the subject */,
  "medical_history" TEXT /* Text field for any relevant medical history */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the subject */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_site_id FOREIGN KEY ("site_id") REFERENCES sites ("site_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_label FOREIGN KEY ("label") REFERENCES diagnoses ("diagnosis_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sites" (
  "site_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each site */,
  "site_name" TEXT /* Name of the site */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the site */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the site */,
  "established_date" TEXT /* Date when the site was established */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the site director */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the site */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website URL of the site */,
  "research_areas" TEXT /* Text field for the main research areas of the site */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("site_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "scans" (
  "scan_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each scan */,
  "subject_id" TEXT /* ID of the subject the scan belongs to */,
  "resolution" REAL /* Resolution of the scan */,
  "noise" REAL /* Noise level of the scan */,
  "bias" REAL /* Bias correction of the scan */,
  "tiv" REAL /* Total Intracranial Volume (TIV) */,
  "csf" REAL /* Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) volume */,
  "gmv" REAL /* Gray Matter Volume (GMV) */,
  "wmv" REAL /* White Matter Volume (WMV) */,
  "thickness" REAL /* Cortical Thickness */,
  "thickness_std" REAL /* Standard Deviation of Cortical Thickness */,
  "scan_date" TEXT /* Date when the scan was performed */,
  "scanner_model" TEXT /* Model of the scanner used */,
  "protocol_version" TEXT /* Version of the scanning protocol used */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Quality score of the scan */,
  "reviewed_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who reviewed the scan */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the scan was reviewed */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* Path to the scan file on the server */,
  "file_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the scan file for integrity checks */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("scan_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scans_reviewed_by FOREIGN KEY ("reviewed_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_scans_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "diagnoses" (
  "diagnosis_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each diagnosis type */,
  "diagnosis_name" TEXT /* Name of the diagnosis type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the diagnosis type */,
  "criterion" TEXT /* Text field for the diagnostic criterion used */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the diagnosis */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("diagnosis_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, clinician, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hash of the user's password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, suspended, deleted) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Reference to the site where the data was collected
